But it was the authentication we wanted to make sure that all of the actions that are performed right by the users are actually traced back to those users that triggered those actions.
We didn't want to have an agent that would have simply access to everything and could do any kind of action at any point.

And the only thing that we would see in the trace would be an agent made that made that action, but we wouldn't know, we wouldn't know why that's why it was, it was super important to to be able to tie it back to users.
And that's what we did.
And also we figured that task oriented MCPs are a little too granular so they don't maximize the chances of success for AI agents.
